citations.note: 2.1 This Licence governs the use, copying, modification, Conveying of Covered Source and Products, and the Making of Products (no non-commercial restriction)
Section 4: You may Make Products, and/or Convey them
3.2 You may modify Covered Source, other than Notices
3.3 You may Convey modified Covered Source
Convey means to communicate to the public or distribute
3.1 You may copy and Convey verbatim copies of Covered Source, in any medium, provided You retain all Notices
Section 4: You may Make Products, and/or Convey them
2.1 governs use, copying, modification ... (broad grant encompasses private use)
2.4 This Licence is not intended to restrict fair use, fair dealing, or any other similar right

7.1 each Licensor hereby grants to You a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free, irrevocable ... patent license to Make, have Made, use, offer to sell, sell, import, and otherwise transfer the Covered Source and Products
7.2 patent retaliation: rights terminate on patent litigation
Notice includes copyright, acknowledgement and trademark notices
3.1 provided You retain all Notices
3.3(a) retain Notices as required in subsection 3.2
3.3(b) add a Notice to the modified Covered Source stating that You have modified it, with the date and brief description of how You have modified it
3.2 You irrevocably undertake to make that modified Covered Source available from a Source Location should You Convey a Product
Including Covered Source in a larger work is modifying the Covered Source, and the larger work becomes modified Covered Source (strong reciprocal scope)

Section 4: You may Make Products, and/or Convey them, provided that You either provide each recipient with a copy of the Complete Source or ensure that each recipient is notified of the Source Location of the Complete Source
That Complete Source is Covered Source, and You must accordingly satisfy Your obligations set out in subsection 3.3

conditions.commentary: Strongly reciprocal: the entire Complete Source (the set of all Source necessary to Make the Product, except Available Components with adequate sourcing information) must be available under CERN-OHL-S. Unlike CERN-OHL-W, there is no External Material carve-out.
Network/remote-use disclosure is not a natural concept for physical hardware products and is not addressed by CERN-OHL-S.

citations.note: Including Covered Source in a larger work is modifying the Covered Source, and the larger work becomes modified Covered Source (strong reciprocal scope at whole-design level)
3.3(d) license the modified Covered Source under the terms and conditions of this Licence
Such modified Covered Source must be licensed as a whole, but excluding Available Components contained in it, which remain licensed under their own applicable licences

conditions.commentary: Strong (whole-design) reciprocity: including Covered Source in a larger work makes the larger work itself modified Covered Source, so the entire design/Product must be licensed under CERN-OHL-S 2.0 (excluding only Available Components). Unlike CERN-OHL-W, there is no External-Material/documented-interface carve-out, and Products themselves must carry the reciprocal obligation.

citations.note: Notice includes copyright, acknowledgement and trademark notices and all notices that refer to this Licence
3.1 provided You retain all Notices
If specified in a Notice, the Product must visibly and securely display the Source Location on it or its packaging or documentation

6.2 EXCLUSION AND LIMITATION OF LIABILITY -- The Licensor shall, to the maximum extent permitted by law, have no liability for direct, indirect, special, incidental, consequential, exemplary, punitive or other damages
6.1 DISCLAIMER OF WARRANTY -- The Covered Source and any Products are provided 'as is' and any express or implied warranties ... are disclaimed
This disclaimer of warranty is an essential part of this Licence
8.2 You shall not use any of the name ... image, or logo by which the Licensor or CERN is known, except where needed to comply with section 3

limitations.commentary: No affirmative trademark grant; §8.2 imposes a narrow endorsement/name-use restriction rather than a general denial of trademark rights.
